title: Language-Based Digital Twins for Elderly Cognitive Assistance
abstract: arXiv:2606.27334v1 Announce Type: new  Abstract: Digital twins have emerged as a promising paradigm for personalized healthcare, enabling modeling of individual behavior and health trajectories. In cognitive health, early detection of Mild Cognitive Impairment (MCI) remains challenging, where language and conversational patterns serve as non-invasive biomarkers. In this work, we propose a language-based digital twin framework that leverages large language models (LLMs) to mimic the conversational behavior of elderly individuals by incorporating stylometric cues and contextual metadata. To evaluate fidelity and cognitive consistency, we introduce a multi-head conditional variational autoencoder (cVAE) that jointly measures reconstruction quality and predicts cognitive scores. Experiments on the I-CONECT dataset show that the digital twin preserves identity-specific characteristics and achieves reconstruction and MoCA prediction errors comparable to real data, while outperforming baselin

Title: Language-Based Digital Twins for Elderly Cognitive Assistance
Abstract: arXiv:2606.27334v1 Announce Type: new  Abstract: Digital twins have emerged as a promising paradigm for personalized healthcare, enabling modeling of individual behavior and health trajectories. In cognitive health, early detection of Mild Cognitive Impairment (MCI) remains challenging, where language and conversational patterns serve as non-invasive biomarkers. In this work, we propose a language-based digital twin framework that leverages large language models (LLMs) to mimic the conversational behavior of elderly individuals by incorporating stylometric cues and contextual metadata. Experiments on the I-CONECT dataset show that the digital twin preserves identity-specific characteristics and achieves reconstruction and MoCA prediction errors comparable to real data, while outperforming baselin
translated_title: 基于语言的老年认知辅助数字孪生系统
translated_abstract: 数字孪生已成为个性化医疗领域的一种有前景的范式，能够模拟个体行为和健康轨迹。在认知健康方面，轻度认知障碍（MCI）的早期检测仍具有挑战性，而语言和对话模式可作为非侵入性生物标志物。本研究提出了一种基于语言的数字孪生框架，利用大型语言模型（LLM）通过整合文体特征和上下文元数据来模拟老年人的对话行为。为评估保真度和认知一致性，我们引入了一种多头条件变分自编码器（cVAE），该模型可联合衡量重建质量并预测认知评分。在I-CONECT数据集上的实验表明，该数字孪生系统保留了特定身份特征，在重建和MoCA预测误差方面达到与真实数据相当的水平，且优于基线模型。
tldr: 本文提出一种基于大型语言模型的数字孪生框架，通过整合文体特征与元数据模拟老年人对话行为，并利用多头条件变分自编码器实现认知评分预测，在MCI早期检测中达到与真实数据相当的保真度。
en_tdlr: This paper proposes a language-based digital twin framework leveraging LLMs to mimic elderly conversational behavior via stylometric cues and metadata, with a multi-head cVAE for cognitive score prediction, achieving fidelity comparable to real data for early MCI detection.
